clans.label: Banu Haqim
clans.bane: When a Banu Haqim tries to Slake one or more Hunger with vitae from another Kindred, they must immediately test for Hunger Frenzy against a Difficulty of 2 + Bane Severity. If they fail this challenge, they will enter a Frenzy and continue gorging on their Kindred meal. If not stopped, they will attempt to drain their victim and commit diablerie. Judgment

clans.label: Brujah
clans.bane: A Brujah must subtract their Bane Severity from their test pool on any challenge they make to resist Fury Frenzy Rebellion

clans.label: Gangrel
clans.bane: Whenever a gangrel enters a Frenzy they gain a number of animal features equal to their Bane Severity. Each feature reduces one Attribute by one point while manifested. Feral Impulses

clans.label: Hecata
clans.bane: When feeding, Hecata may only take harmful drinks that result in blood loss. Unrestrained targets will attempt to escape, and those coerced or willing must succeed in a Stamina + Resolve check against Difficulty 2 + Bane Severity in order not to recoil from the pain. Morbidity

clans.label: Lasombra
clans.bane: Any reflective surface or recording device renders the Lasombra’s image as faded or distorted in some way. Additionally, any time physical interaction based solely on touch is required to operate a modern communication device, the Lasombra must make a test using their Technology Skill vs. a Difficulty of 2 + Bane Severity. Ruthlessness

clans.label: Malkavian
clans.bane: All Malkavians suffer from a derangement. They receive a penalty equal to their Bane Severity to all challenges involving one Attribute category when they suffer a Bestial Failure. This penalty lasts for the scene. Delusions

clans.label: The Ministry
clans.bane: The Ministry have always been sensitive to light. Exposure to direct light, natural or otherwise, causes a Serpent to recoil. They take a penalty equal to their Bane Severity to all challenges while subjected to bright light directed straight at them. Ministry also take twice their Bane Severity in Aggravated Damage (before any reduction from protection) from exposure to sunlight. Transgression

clans.label: Nosferatu
clans.bane: All Nosferatu appear hideous and vile, though not always supernaturally so. Nosferatu suffer a -2 penalty on all mundane social challenges when their natural appearance is visible. Additionally, all attempts by the character to disguise their deformities, including with the use of Disciplines, incur a penalty equal to the character’s Bane Severity. Cryptophilia

clans.label: Ravnos
clans.bane: If a Ravnos does not spend one downtime action per month moving from haven to haven or sleeping in different locations, they take a number of Aggravated Damage equal to their Bane Severity. Tempting Fate

clans.label: Salubri
clans.bane: If a character drinks enough Salubri Blood to Slake at least one Hunger, they must make a Hunger Frenzy test at Difficulty 2 + Bane severity. If they fail, the imbiber continues to feed and cannot stop. The Salubri also has a third eye that is always present. When the Salubri uses a Discipline, the third eye weeps vitae that triggers a Hunger Frenzy test from nearby vampires with Hunger 4 or higher. Affective Empathy

clans.label: Toreador
clans.bane: Beauty is more than a simple, shallow want for the Toreador; an environment that violates their aesthetic sensibilities distracts and chafes to the point of physical detriment. When forced to tolerate such offensive surroundings, a Diva suffers a penalty equal to their Bane Severity on any attempt to use Disciplines in that space. Obsession

clans.label: Tremere
clans.bane: Clan Tremere can no longer subject another vampire to the shackles of the Blood Bond. They are still subject to the Bond themselves. Ghouls and humans can still be Bound, but they must consume their domitor’s vitae an additional number of times equal to their Bane Severity. Perfectionism

clans.label: Tzimisce
clans.bane: Tzimisce are ruled by what they possess. Choose a charge, a group of people, an organization, a physical domain, etc. The Dragon must sleep surrounded by their chosen charge. If they do not, when they Awaken they lose a number of Willpower equal to their Bane Severity. Covetousness

clans.label: Ventrue
clans.bane: Refined in palate as much as in action, the Ventrue are extremely picky eaters. . If a Ventrue attempts to feed from anyone outside their preference, they must spend Willpower equal to their Bane severity or vomit the blood back up. A Blue Blood can tell whether someone fits their preference even at a distance by making a Resolve + Awareness challenge vs. Difficulty 4, or through the use of the Bloodhound Merit. Arrogance

clans.label: Caitiff
clans.bane: Because they lack a clan, the Outcasts generally do not carry a Bane, but are considered socially outcast by much of vampire society. Whether they are Anarch or accepted in a Camarilla Court, a known Caitiff requires 2 additional Support beyond the normal Support required to gain a dot of Status. None

clans.label: Thin-Bloods
clans.bane: Beauty is more than a simple, shallow want for the Toreador; an environment that violates their aesthetic sensibilities distracts and chafes to the point of physical detriment. When forced to tolerate such offensive surroundings, a Diva suffers a penalty equal to their Bane Severity on any attempt to use Disciplines in that space. None
